qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Go flatted parser panics on out-of-range reference indexes
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The advisory is a JavaScript prototype-pollution flaw in `parse()` (`esm/index.js` / `cjs/index.js`) where the attacker-controlled string `"__proto__"` is used as an array index. This finding is a separate Go-only out-of-range panic in `golang/pkg/flatted/flatted.go` caused by unchecked numeric indexes, so it does not identify the advisory's vulnerable code or root cause.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding reaches the advisory's vulnerable JavaScript `parse()` path or its `__proto__`-based prototype-pollution root cause; the only final qualifying finding is a distinct Go parser panic.
evidence: The advisory describes `const tmp = input[value];` in `esm/index.js:29` and `cjs/index.js`, where attacker-controlled `"__proto__"` resolves through the Array prototype chain and leaks a live `Array.prototype` reference.

The session's only final qualifying finding targets `golang/pkg/flatted/flatted.go:229` and `golang/pkg/flatted/flatted.go:239`, where unchecked `strconv.Atoi` results are used as slice indexes and trigger `panic: runtime error: index out of range`.
The finding's demonstrated payload is `[{"a":"999"}]`, which exercises numeric out-of-bounds indexing in the Go port, not the advisory's `"__proto__"` lookup in the JavaScript parser.
